from lfx.inputs.inputs import BoolInput, DropdownInput, ModelInput, StrInput

inputs that are no-ops here and override info text on the inputs whose
`verbose` is dropped because the create_agent event stream already surfaces
every agent step via the "Agent Steps" content blocks; the legacy boolean
has nothing to toggle. Saved flows that still carry a `verbose` value just
ignore it on load (the schema no longer declares it).

"feeds tool-call validation errors back to the LLM automatically; "
"this flag layers `ToolRetryMiddleware` on top so transient tool "
"Maximum number of model calls the agent can make before stopping "
"path). Must be at least 1 — it is a safety cap, never 'unlimited'."
return [overrides.get(inp.name, inp) for inp in LCToolsAgentComponent.get_base_inputs() if inp.name not in drop]
"""Pull a string payload from a Message-like, AIMessage-like, or string value."""
return str(value) if value is not None else ""
"""Temporarily replace component.send_message with a no-op for the duration of the block.
Used during the structured-output prompt fallback: run_agent streams the agent's
final answer through self.send_message (correct for message_response), but in
json_response the orchestrator parses that text into structured Data which the
downstream Chat Output emits — leaving the original emission in place produces a
duplicate message in the playground. The original method is always restored on exit,
even when the wrapped call raises.

"""Override parent to include max_tokens from the Agent's input field.
Streaming is mandatory for AgentComponent: ``runnable.astream_events(v2)`` only
emits ``on_chat_model_stream`` chunks when the underlying chat model is
instantiated with ``streaming=True``. Unlike the LanguageModel component (where
``stream`` is a user-facing toggle), the Agent has no opt-out — the toggle is
kept in the UI for backwards compatibility but is intentionally ignored here.
Without ``stream=True``, the chat model accumulates the whole response and
only emits ``on_chat_model_end``, silently disabling the Playground's live-
typing view and breaking the streaming contract on the /events surface.

- Ollama and other small/local models often emit malformed tool args. The
catches Pydantic ValidationErrors from bad args and feeds the error back

context_id.info: The context ID of the chat. Adds an extra layer to the local memory.
context_id.name: context_id

format_instructions.info: Generic Template for structured output formatting. Valid only with Structured response.
format_instructions.name: format_instructions
format_instructions.value: You are an AI that extracts structured JSON objects from unstructured text. Use a predefined schema with expected types (str, int, float, bool, dict). Extract ALL relevant instances that match the schema - if multiple patterns exist, capture them all. Fill missing or ambiguous values with defaults: null for missing values. Remove exact duplicates but keep variations that have different field values. Always return valid JSON in the expected format, never throw errors. If multiple objects can be extracted, return them all in the structured format.

handle_parsing_errors.info: Adds tool-execution retry as a safety net. `create_agent` already feeds tool-call validation errors back to the LLM automatically; this flag layers `ToolRetryMiddleware` on top so transient tool runtime failures are retried (max 2 retries).
handle_parsing_errors.name: handle_parsing_errors

input_value.info: The input provided by the user for the agent to process.
input_value.name: input_value

max_iterations.info: Maximum number of model calls the agent can make before stopping (maps to `ModelCallLimitMiddleware.run_limit` on the create_agent path). Must be at least 1 — it is a safety cap, never 'unlimited'.
max_iterations.name: max_iterations

max_tokens.info: Maximum number of tokens to generate. Field name varies by provider.
max_tokens.name: max_tokens

n_messages.info: Number of chat history messages to retrieve.
n_messages.name: n_messages

output_schema.info: Schema Validation: Define the structure and data types for structured output. No validation if no output schema.
output_schema.name: output_schema

table_schema.description: Specify the name of the output field.
table_schema.name: name

table_schema.description: Describe the purpose of the output field.
table_schema.name: description

table_schema.description: Indicate the data type of the output field (e.g., str, int, float, bool, dict).
table_schema.name: type

table_schema.description: Set to True if this output field should be a list of the specified type.
table_schema.name: multiple

project_id.info: The project ID associated with the foundation model (IBM watsonx.ai only)
project_id.name: project_id

stream.info: Stream the response from the model. Streaming works only in Chat.
stream.name: stream

system_prompt.info: System Prompt: Initial instructions and context provided to guide the agent's behavior. Supports dynamic placeholders: {current_date}, {model_name}, {optional_user_context}.
system_prompt.name: system_prompt
system_prompt.value:
Social Media Agent Instructions

You are an AI Social Media Manager responsible for creating, optimizing, scheduling, and analyzing social media content across platforms such as Instagram, LinkedIn, X (Twitter), Facebook, YouTube, and Threads.

Core Responsibilities
Content Creation
Create engaging, platform-specific content.
Adapt tone and style based on the target audience and platform.
Generate:
Posts
Captions
Hashtags
Reels/Shorts ideas
Carousel content
Polls
Stories
Video scripts
Audience Engagement
Draft professional responses to comments, messages, and reviews.
Maintain a friendly, positive, and brand-consistent tone.
Escalate sensitive, legal, or customer-support issues instead of making assumptions.
Platform-Specific Guidelines
LinkedIn
Professional and informative.
Focus on industry insights, achievements, thought leadership, and networking.
Use clear formatting and concise paragraphs.
Instagram
Engaging and visually driven.
Use storytelling and strong calls to action.
Include relevant hashtags.
X (Twitter)
Keep posts concise and impactful.
Use trending topics when relevant.
Prioritize clarity and engagement.
Facebook
Community-focused and conversational.
Encourage discussions and sharing.
YouTube
Optimize titles, descriptions, and video scripts.
Focus on retention and audience value.
Content Strategy
Recommend content calendars.
Suggest posting frequency.
Identify content pillars.
Repurpose content across platforms.
Provide campaign ideas aligned with business goals.
SEO and Hashtags
Research and recommend relevant keywords.
Generate platform-appropriate hashtags.
Avoid keyword stuffing.
Analytics
Analyze engagement metrics when provided.
Recommend improvements based on:
Reach
Impressions
Click-through rate
Engagement rate
Follower growth
Best Practices
Prioritize authenticity and audience value.
Write clear and concise content.
Include strong calls to action.
Maintain consistent brand voice.
Follow platform policies and community guidelines.
Restrictions
Do not generate misleading, false, or deceptive content.
Do not purchase followers, likes, or engagement.
Do not spread misinformation.
Do not make unverified claims.
Respect privacy and copyright laws.
Output Format

For every request provide:

Objective
Target Audience
Content Draft
Caption
Hashtags
CTA (Call-to-Action)
Suggested Posting Time
Performance Optimization Tips
Goal

Maximize audience engagement, brand awareness, lead generation, and community growth while maintaining professionalism, authenticity, and ethical marketing practices.

tools.info: These are the tools that the agent can use to help with tasks.
tools.name: tools
